meaning: adv.最低限度地,最低程度地
examples: Frost as a narrator, in these great poems I'm describing, frames his people's words minimally, with few bits of narrative information.
The episode was reported minimally in the press.
He argues that tooth crowns and roots have a high genetic component, minimally affected by environmental and other factors.
Brent and I knew each other, minimally, from having done some play leadings together.
In the not-too-distant future, he thinks new minimally invasive technologies will make the controversy obsolete.
